reviews.review_msg: His lectures might be boring to some of the students. Because his voice is not high nor enthusiasm. But he will write his notes while he is speaking. And that is the most important key point for this corse!!!! It helps you know what is the steps when he solving the problems, what is he talking and how is he thinking, and how you should learn MATH.

He is an excellent teacher: clear, organized, informative. And there is something charming in his attitude: he is not really snob, but he knows to keep a distance - he is hot and cold at the same time. He makes me feel like it was in my childhood, when I tried to be a good boy. I know that other students feel similar. Nice experience.
Very nice prof. Definitely he loves teaching. Classes are not tedious. In fact, I spend quite lot of time on assignments. Fair marking. Midterms are passable.
Likerecommend this prof. midterms follow both assignment questions and class notes. marking is very fair. classes are well structured. he gives clear examples to the course is not stuffy.
Literally, one of the best professors at SFU. Very clear on what he talks about, well organized notes. I agree with prev. posts, he seems to love lecturing. Straightforward exams, fair marking.

Overall, he is a good prof. He is maybe not talented in teaching but really passionate and makes lots of effort into the course for students. Lectures are more theoretical, while tutorials are for problem solving. Assignments are very time consuming and expect you to use a Math software like Maple. Exams are fair where the class avg is about 65%.
Highly recommend. He knows how to teach and he makes it interesting. Be careful, he asks quit lot of 'must understand' questions, definitions, theorems, etc...

really nice man. He is not the kind of skilled math teacher, but he will answer you question in patience. make few mistake in class but not a big deal, very clear class note, and very nice on your mark. Most importantly, very easy exam, almost everything you have done from notes and assignment.
